subject: Chemistry
title: Fluorescence: Glowing Under UV
description: A 60-second lesson on Fluorescence: Glowing Under UV.

hook.text: Some substances absorb invisible UV light and re-emit it as visible color. They glow under blacklight.

buildup.text: UV photons excite electrons to high energy levels. They lose some energy as heat, then emit lower-energy visible light.

discovery.text: Fluorescein dye absorbs UV and emits bright green. Quinine in tonic water glows blue. Scorpions glow cyan.

twist.text: Laundry detergent contains fluorescent whiteners. They absorb UV and emit blue light, making white clothes look brighter.

climax.text: GFP (green fluorescent protein) from jellyfish earned a Nobel Prize. Scientists use it to tag and track proteins in living cells.

punchline.text: Absorb invisible light, emit visible glow. Nature's own blacklight show.

quiz.question: Why do fluorescent substances glow under UV light?
options: They absorb UV energy and re-emit it as lower-energy visible light
UV light reflects off their surface more intensely
They produce chemical reactions that generate light
